scenario: A fintech runs 3000 instances per day. In some instances service task A charge-customer is currently active; in others it has already completed. The target process definition adds a new compensation boundary event to task A. The team must choose the migration plan that ensures a compensation subscription exists for as many instances as possible after migration completes.
question: Which mapping correctly migrates the in-flight instances so a compensation subscription is created for the instances still active on service task A?

options.text: Map service task A to A so a compensation subscription is opened on completion of the active element A in the target process; instances that already finished A receive no new compensation subscription
Map service task A to B so the engine retroactively attaches the new compensation boundary event to every prior execution of A and opens compensation subscriptions for already-completed instances
Provide no mapping for service task A so the engine adds a default compensation subscription to every active element instance in the source process regardless of its element id
Map service task A to A and add a variable instruction compensateOnMigration set to true so the engine immediately opens compensation subscriptions for already-completed instances of A

a.text: Correct. When a compensation boundary event is added during migration, a new compensation subscription is opened only for activity instances that are currently active or have not started yet. Mapping A to A is enough; finished instances of A do not receive a new compensation subscription.

b.text: Incorrect. The engine never opens compensation subscriptions retroactively for activity instances that have already completed. Mapping A to B would also change the active element instead of preserving its identity for the migration.

c.text: Incorrect. A migration plan must map every active element. Without a mapping for service task A the validation step rejects the command, so no compensation subscriptions are opened during migration at all.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no compensateOnMigration variable contract in process instance migration. Compensation subscriptions for already-finished activities cannot be opened by any migration plan, regardless of variable instructions provided.

explanation: Compensation boundary events follow a special rule during migration: a new compensation subscription is opened only for activity instances that are currently active or have not started yet, never for activity instances that already finished before the migration ran.
